---
title: "Other Challenges"
subtitle: "Beyond the two set challenges — a deeper menu to explore or run as alternates."
description: "Additional hands-on AI challenges for pain scientists, plus an advanced agentic track for Claude Code / Codex / Gemini CLI."
---

<svg viewBox="0 0 800 220" width="100%" style="max-height:220px;display:block;margin:0 0 1.2rem;border-radius:16px;background:linear-gradient(135deg,#f6f8fc,#eef3fb);border:1px solid #e2e8f4">
  <defs>
    <linearGradient id="g-other" x1="0" y1="0" x2="1" y2="1">
      <stop offset="0%" stop-color="#0891b2"/>
      <stop offset="55%" stop-color="#e11d6b"/>
      <stop offset="100%" stop-color="#7c3aed"/>
    </linearGradient>
  </defs>
  <!-- faint connecting lattice behind the arena -->
  <g stroke="url(#g-other)" stroke-width="1" opacity="0.22">
    <line x1="120" y1="60" x2="260" y2="150"/>
    <line x1="260" y1="150" x2="400" y2="70"/>
    <line x1="400" y1="70" x2="540" y2="160"/>
    <line x1="540" y1="160" x2="680" y2="80"/>
    <line x1="120" y1="160" x2="260" y2="60"/>
    <line x1="400" y1="160" x2="540" y2="60"/>
  </g>
  <!-- arena of rounded challenge tiles -->
  <g fill="#ffffff">
    <rect x="70"  y="70"  width="90" height="80" rx="14" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1.5"/>
    <rect x="180" y="95"  width="90" height="80" rx="14" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1.5"/>
    <rect x="290" y="55"  width="90" height="80" rx="14" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1.5"/>
    <rect x="400" y="100" width="90" height="80" rx="14" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1.5"/>
    <rect x="510" y="60"  width="90" height="80" rx="14" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1.5"/>
    <rect x="620" y="95"  width="90" height="80" rx="14" stroke="#7c3aed" stroke-width="2"/>          <!-- advanced -->
  </g>
  <!-- motif glyphs inside the tiles -->
  <g fill="none" stroke-width="2">
    <polyline points="86,128 102,112 118,122 134,96" stroke="#64748b"/>
    <path d="M196 150 q22 -34 44 0" stroke="#64748b"/>
    <path d="M306 110 l16 -18 16 18 -16 18 z" stroke="#0891b2"/>
    <path d="M416 150 q22 -30 44 0 M438 124 v34" stroke="#64748b"/>
    <circle cx="555" cy="100" r="16" stroke="#e11d6b"/><circle cx="555" cy="100" r="5" fill="#e11d6b" stroke="none"/>
    <path d="M636 150 l14 -16 14 10 14 -22" stroke="#7c3aed"/>
  </g>
  <g fill="#475569">
    <circle cx="120" cy="60" r="2.5"/>
    <circle cx="400" cy="70" r="2.5"/>
    <circle cx="680" cy="80" r="2.5"/>
    <circle cx="540" cy="160" r="2.5" opacity="0.7"/>
  </g>
  <circle cx="334" cy="64" r="3" fill="#b91c1c"/>
</svg>

<div class="meta-row">
  <span class="pill cyan">14 challenges</span>
  <span class="pill rose">run as alternates</span>
  <span class="pill teal">35 min each</span>
  <span class="pill violet">advanced agentic track</span>
</div>

These are the rest of the menu — beyond the [two set challenges](index.qmd) we run live. Each follows the same format: a short setup, a starter artifact you can paste straight into any chatbot, a few rounds of iteration, and a quick share-out. Use them as alternates, as warm-ups, or as something to try on your own afterward.

**New to AI?** Start anywhere on the first grid — these assume no prior experience. **Already comfortable with these models?** Skip to the advanced track and wire up some agents.

## Core & foundational challenges

<div class="card-grid">

<a class="lafb-card" href="picture-this.qmd">
<h3>📊 Picture This</h3>
<p>Rescue an ugly data table and turn it into the most informative figure in the room. Beginner-friendly, instant payoff.</p>
</a>

<a class="lafb-card" href="plain-language-gauntlet.qmd">
<h3>🗣️ Plain-Language Gauntlet</h3>
<p>One dense methods paragraph, three very different audiences.</p>
</a>

<a class="lafb-card" href="hypothesis-roulette.qmd">
<h3>🎰 Hypothesis Roulette</h3>
<p>A surprising null result walks in. Spin up five testable mechanisms.</p>
</a>

<a class="lafb-card" href="model-horserace.qmd">
<h3>🐎 Model Horserace</h3>
<p>Same task, five different free AIs — compare what each one does well.</p>
</a>

<a class="lafb-card" href="the-rebuttal.qmd">
<h3>⚖️ The Rebuttal</h3>
<p>Reviewer 2 was brutal. Draft a response that brings the editor around.</p>
</a>

<a class="lafb-card" href="irb-speed-round.qmd">
<h3>⏱️ IRB Speed Round</h3>
<p>Draft a risks &amp; benefits section fast — and catch the AI when it bluffs.</p>
</a>

<a class="lafb-card" href="experiment-generator.qmd">
<h3>🧪 Experiment Generator</h3>
<p>From a single finding to six experiments that nail the mechanism.</p>
</a>

<a class="lafb-card" href="rapid-review.qmd">
<h3>📚 Rapid Review</h3>
<p>A 2-page evidence brief with real references, built in minutes.</p>
</a>

<a class="lafb-card" href="flash-debate.qmd">
<h3>🥊 Flash Debate</h3>
<p>Build the evidence case for a contested claim — then argue it.</p>
</a>

<a class="lafb-card" href="meet-the-speaker.qmd">
<h3>🤝 Meet the Speaker</h3>
<p>Walk into a visit knowing exactly who to meet and what to say.</p>
</a>

<a class="lafb-card" href="policy-wonk.qmd">
<h3>🏛️ Policy Wonk</h3>
<p>The general version of the comment challenge: turn any dense rule change into an effective public comment.</p>
</a>

</div>

## Advanced track — agentic tools (Claude Code / Codex / Gemini CLI)

These three move beyond the chat box. You will drive command-line agents that read files, run code, and coordinate with each other. Come with a tool installed (see the AI Toolkit) and some curiosity about how multiple agents work together.

<div class="card-grid">

<a class="lafb-card" href="advanced/reviewer-4.qmd">
<h3>🧠 Reviewer 4</h3>
<p>Stand up a multi-agent grant clinic that critiques your R01 like a study section.</p>
</a>

<a class="lafb-card" href="advanced/who-are-my-friends.qmd">
<h3>🔭 Who Are My Friends?</h3>
<p>Design a system that finds the reviewers who will appreciate your paper.</p>
</a>

<a class="lafb-card" href="advanced/junkyard-dog.qmd">
<h3>🦴 Junkyard Dog</h3>
<p>Sniff out open pain datasets, fetch them, and wire them together with code.</p>
</a>

</div>

<div class="box tip"><span class="box-label">Before you dive in</span> Need a model and an account? See the <a href="../intro/tools.qmd">AI Toolkit</a> for setup. Want to know how the 35-minute format works? Read <a href="../intro/overview.qmd">The Workshop</a>. Looking for the two challenges we run live? They're on <a href="index.qmd">The Challenges</a>.</div>
